name: Metabase
what_it_does: Open-source business intelligence and analytics platform with a REST API for managing questions, dashboards, users, and permissions, and for embedding analytics into applications.
use_cases: Programmatically creating and updating questions and dashboards for multi-tenant analytics
Embedding Metabase charts and dashboards in external applications via signed tokens
Automating user and permission management for self-service analytics teams
Triggering question refreshes and exporting results in agent-driven reporting workflows
Managing database connections and data model metadata via API
not_for: Real-time analytics requiring sub-second query results
Advanced ML or predictive analytics (BI tool, not ML platform)
Organizations needing enterprise-grade row-level security without Metabase Pro
Teams wanting a fully managed SaaS BI solution without self-hosting overhead
best_when: You want an open-source, self-hosted BI tool with embedding capabilities and a REST API for automation, and your team has the infrastructure to run it.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-scale BI, advanced ML integration, or want fully managed SaaS with no operational overhead.

auth.notes: Session-based auth (POST /api/session) returns a token. API keys available in newer versions. No OAuth or fine-grained scope control — auth is user-level with Metabase's own permission system.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: completely free; Metabase Cloud Starter: free for up to 5 users
pricing.notes: Open source version is fully functional. Pro/Enterprise add SSO, advanced permissions, and SLA. Cloud hosted available if you don't want to self-host.

performance.rate_limits: No documented rate limits; depends on self-hosted infrastructure

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns standard HTTP codes with JSON error details. Error messages are functional but documentation of the API is less comprehensive than commercial alternatives.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Question/dashboard creation is not idempotent — creates duplicates. Updates by ID are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Session tokens expire after 14 days by default — agents must re-authenticate before expiry
The API is not formally versioned — updates can break automation between Metabase versions
Card (question) IDs are integers auto-assigned — agents must store IDs after creation
Embedding tokens are short-lived JWTs signed with Metabase's embedding secret — regenerate per request
API calls that modify dashboards require knowing all existing cards/positions — partial updates not supported
